texts.orig: · Volatile, but not too much. Optimum b.p. 70 - 120°C

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Should dissolve polymer, solution can form gel at > 5 wt.%
texts.text: Should dissolve polymer, solution can form gel at > 5 wt.%

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As high surface porosity of porous membrane as possible
texts.text: As high surface porosity of porous membrane as possible

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Stable against the pressure and temperature of the separation process
texts.text: Stable against the pressure and temperature of the separation process

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Suitable for membrane envelope formation by glueing or melting
texts.text: Suitable for membrane envelope formation by glueing or melting

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Find lowest concentration of polymer solution when polymer film is still formed
texts.text: Find lowest concentration of polymer solution when polymer film is still formed

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Observe the formed polymer layer and enjoy colors
texts.text: Observe the formed polymer layer and enjoy colors

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Test the membrane and compare results to properties of the polymer

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Thin-film composite (TFC) membrane fabrication: polymer → membrane → module
texts.text: Thin-film composite (TFC) membrane fabrication: polymer → membrane → module

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Separations processes based on polymeric membranes are highly accepted by industry.
texts.text: Separations processes based on polymeric membranes are highly accepted by industry.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Polymeric membranes are not an ultimate tool for solving any separation problem: process design should/could combine membranes with conventional methods such as absorption, adsorption, etc.
texts.text: Polymeric membranes are not an ultimate tool for solving any separation problem: process design should/could combine membranes with conventional methods such as absorption, adsorption, etc.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Multilayer design of TFC membranes gives developer flexibility in a material choice. Each layer is serving specific task: mechanical stability, permeate drainage, smooth support, permeance and selectivity, protection.
texts.text: Multilayer design of TFC membranes gives developer flexibility in a material choice. Each layer is serving specific task: mechanical stability, permeate drainage, smooth support, permeance and selectivity, protection.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. TFC membranes give the possibility for industrial application of experimental materials.
texts.text: TFC membranes give the possibility for industrial application of experimental materials.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. The new generation of TFC membranes developed within the BioCoMem is based on nearly 50 years of experience in membrane R&D and shows the way forward for the use of new materials with unique selective properties (polymers, carbons, ionic liquids, porous sorbents, etc.).
texts.text: The new generation of TFC membranes developed within the BioCoMem is based on nearly 50 years of experience in membrane R&D and shows the way forward for the use of new materials with unique selective properties (polymers, carbons, ionic liquids, porous sorbents, etc.).
